title: populus subintegerrima
intro: Populus subintegerrima, also known as the trembling aspen, is a deciduous tree species native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its large leaves that quiver in the wind. The medicinal properties of Populus subintegerrima lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, providing anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Populus subintegerrima include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its bark can also be dried and powdered for later use.
